messages.content: integrate the sentences: According to the resource-based view (RBV), IT capabilities are a source of competitive advantage that cannot be easily replicated by competitors, unlike IT resources. However, over time, some IT capabilities may become standardized and diminishes their distinctiveness, eventually becoming mere IT resources.

The resource-based view (RBV) suggests that IT capabilities are a competitive advantage that is challenging to replicate, unlike IT resources, which could eventually become standardized and lose their distinctiveness.

combine: According to the resource-based view (RBV), IT capabilities are a source of competitive advantage that cannot be easily replicated by competitors, unlike IT resources. However, over time, some IT capabilities may become standardized and diminishes their distinctiveness, eventually becoming mere IT resources.

IT capabilities are initially viewed as a source of competitive advantage that sets one apart from competitors, yet the resource-based view (RBV) shows that over time, these capabilities may become standardized, losing their distinctiveness and being relegated to the status of mere IT resources.

edit: According to the resource-based view (RBV), IT capabilities are a source of competitive advantage that cannot be easily replicated by competitors, unlike IT resources, however, over time, some IT capabilities may become standardized and diminishes their distinctiveness, eventually becoming mere IT resources.

According to the resource-based view (RBV), IT capabilities provide a competitive advantage that is difficult for competitors to imitate, unlike IT resources. However, it is important to note that certain IT capabilities may become standardized over time, reducing their distinctiveness and relegating them to the status of ordinary IT resources.

Nevertheless, some IT capabilities might become standardized over time and lose their distinctiveness, eventually becoming typical IT resources.

edit and combine : According to the resource-based view (RBV), IT capabilities are a source of competitive advantage that cannot be easily replicated by competitors, in contrast to IT resources. According to the resource-based view (RBV), IT capabilities are a competitive advantage that is difficult for competitors to replicate, unlike IT resources. Despite this, over time, certain IT capabilities may lose their distinctiveness and become standardized, ultimately resulting in their reclassification as IT resources.

sumarraize: According to the resource-based view (RBV), IT capabilities are a source of competitive advantage that cannot be easily replicated by competitors, in contrast to IT resources. However, some IT capabilities might gradually lose their distinctiveness and become standardized over time, leading to their reclassification as IT resources.

RBV suggests that IT capabilities are a competitive advantage that sets one apart from competitors, while IT resources are easy to imitate. However, certain IT capabilities may lose their distinctiveness over time and become standardized and reclassified as IT resources.

edit: Physical IT infrastructure has long been viewed as a capability in most studies, the exponential growth of digital technologies, including cloud computing, has led to questions as to whether this traditional viewpoint remains valid in the context of hospitals, particularly during the process of DT.

While physical IT infrastructure has historically been regarded as a key capability in various studies, the rapid development of digital technologies, such as cloud computing, has raised questions about the continued relevance of this perspective, particularly in hospitals during the process of digital transformation (DT).
